title: Deep Dive: Rostam Mahabadi — Genie AI Agency (2pm Meeting Prep)
content:
# Rostam Mahabadi — Deep Dive and Meeting Prep

**Email:** rostammahabadi@gmail.com / hello@g3nie.ai
**LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/rostam-mahabadi
**GitHub:** github.com/Rostammahabadi (117 repos, GitHub Pro)
**Website:** g3nie.ai / rostam.dev
**Location:** Austin, TX
**Meeting:** 2:00 PM today

## Who He Is

Founder and CEO of Genie AI Agency (g3nie.ai), a premium AI product studio founded in 2024. Builds production-ready AI agents, RAG pipelines, and LLM integrations for businesses. Not a chatbot shop: he integrates AI into entire business workflows.

## Career Path

- Turing School of Software & Design (coding bootcamp, 2020)
- 4 years software engineering (Randall Reilly as Senior SWE)
- First AI project saved employer $175K/year
- Gauntlet AI Fellowship (2025): intensive 100-hour/week, 3-month program. Embedded with Series B companies ($3B valuations), government contractors, cancer research facilities
- Founded Genie AI Agency (2024)

## His Clients

- **Function Health** — multi-step health agents translating biomarkers into guidance
- **Oncology Resource Group** — RAG systems for 100-200 page oncology reports
- **WinChargebacks** — production-ready AI workflow with Postgres, SSO, CI
- **Satellite imagery company** — conversational AI replacing dashboards
- Government contractors and Series B companies via Gauntlet

## How He Sells (90-95% Close Rate)

- Radical transparency: gives full architectural blueprint upfront before signing
- Presents 3-4 AI approaches with architecture diagrams
- 'Domain Discovery' methodology: stakeholder interviews, workflow observation, data review, metrics baselining before any coding
- Pricing tied to measurable business outcomes
- Premium positioning, no public pricing

## Tech Stack

TypeScript, Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, LangGraph, OpenAI APIs, Vercel AI SDK, pgvector, PostgreSQL, CopilotKit, Mastra

## Side Projects

- **Decord** (decord.app) — HIPAA-compliant screen recording for macOS
- **VoiceScribe** — Speech-to-Text with local AI (Swift)
- **n8n-nodes-square** — Square integration for n8n automation
- **Vantheon** — unclear if parent company or separate venture

## His Values

- Community-driven: 'People helped me along the way. It's only right I help people along the way.'
- Anti-hype: distinguishes real vs. fake AI engineers
- Continuous learner: reviews GitHub trending daily, scans arXiv
- Also a certified professional skating instructor (PSA)

## What to Talk About

1. **Referral partnership** — he's in Austin, you're in the Northeast. No geographic overlap. Send overflow work each other's way.
2. **His Domain Discovery process** — how he runs stakeholder interviews and scopes projects before coding. Worth adopting.
3. **Single market segment strategy** — Nelson Merchan just advised you to niche down. Ask Rostam how he picked his niche and what he'd focus on.
4. **Equity/accelerator model** — you're exploring the Acquisition.com model. Does he do equity deals or strictly fee-for-service?
5. **Education sector** — pitch the education opportunities (language barriers, teacher admin burden, superintendent software). Does he see AI in education as a market?
6. **Scaling from solo to team** — he's solo, you're hiring engineers. Compare notes on when to stay lean vs. build a team.
7. **The Danbury ecosystem** — Chamber of Commerce AI specialist role, WestConn partnership, Mike Koshny's co-working space. Any of this interesting to him as a remote collaborator?

title: Meeting Notes: Nelson Merchan — Strategy, Contacts, and Opportunities
content:
# Nelson Merchan Meeting Notes

## Business Strategy and Positioning Advice

- **Focus on a single market segment** rather than spreading across multiple opportunities. Example: a website developer who only serves plumbers charges premium rates.
- Set clear revenue objectives with timeline (August target, December growth goal)
- **Partner with domain experts who lack tech skills.** Combine their industry knowledge with your AI/software capabilities. Avoid trying to be expert in every field.
- **Maintain premium pricing standards.** Don't undersell services to accommodate lower budgets. Work only with clients who value and can afford your expertise.

## Key Networking Contacts and Opportunities

**Chamber of Commerce Director (Natasha/Maura)**
- Expressed interest in AI specialist for leadership program
- Monthly learner sessions opportunity
- July B2B event at Danbury High School (largest in CT)

**Mike Koshny — Hacker Space Owner**
- Main Street location next to library (former bank building)
- 25,000 sq ft co-working space planned
- Perfect timing for office space search (schedule May tour)

**WestConn President Jesse**
- Young, AI-savvy leader
- Recommended connection for educational partnerships

## Current Business Expansion

- Two operational branches:
  1. Literacy/education (libraries, schools)
  2. Accelerator/equity partnerships (Acquisition.com model)
- Office space search timeline: within 1-3 months
- Hiring more engineers and foot soldiers
- Piloting superintendent software at Woodstock, CT today

## Education Sector Insights and Opportunities

- **Language barrier challenges in schools.** Students with strong skills held back by English proficiency. Current solutions (Rosetta Stone) inadequate.
- **Teacher administrative burden.** Drowning in emails, paperwork vs actual teaching. AI solutions could free up teaching time.
- **Peer-to-peer impact** more effective than adult-to-student messaging. 11th grade presentations planned for Danbury schools.
- **New Milford High School** potential opportunity. Superintendent encourages innovation (ski club example). Contact available through athletics director.

title: Meeting Prep: James Mustich, Iona University (2:30pm)
content:
# Meeting Prep: James Mustich
## Senior Advisor to the President, Iona University
*April 9, 2026 at 2:30pm*

---

## Who He Is
- Senior Advisor to the President at Iona University (New Rochelle, NY) since Feb 2024
- His job: research trends in higher ed and propose pilot projects to the president
- Former Head of Apple Books
- 14 years at Barnes & Noble: VP Digital Products, VP Business Innovation, founding editor of B&N Review
- Author of "1,000 Books to Read Before You Die" (took 14 years to write)
- Started as a bookseller at an independent bookstore in Briarcliff Manor, NY in the 1980s
- Not a traditional academic. He is an innovator and lifelong learner.

---

## Why This Meeting Matters
Iona is one of the most AI-forward universities in the region:
- **AI@Iona:** campuswide AI initiative through Gabelli Center for Teaching & Learning
- **AI Micro Credential** with Yonkers Public Schools (training K-12 teachers on AI)
- **AI minor** launched Sept 2024
- **Fall conference** "Future of Teaching in the AI Age" drew hundreds of educators from 11 colleges, 11 school districts, 20+ high schools
- **Presidential Speaker Series** on AI ethics and philosophy
- **Summer 2026 program** with industry partners on AI + workforce
- **"Education in Real Life"** program bridging the AI gap

Mustich is the person who finds new things and brings them to the president. If NGL fits, he is the door.

---

## The Angle
Iona trains teachers on AI. NGL trains students on AI. Together, we cover the whole pipeline.
- Iona already works with Yonkers Public Schools on teacher AI training. NGL could be the student-facing piece.
- Their fall conference drew 11 districts and 20+ high schools. NGL could present or run a student workshop.
- NGL fills the K-8 gap: who is preparing students before they reach college?

---

## Questions to Ask
1. What is the biggest gap you see in AI education right now?
2. How is Iona thinking about reaching younger students (K-8) before college?
3. The micro credential program with Yonkers: how is that going? What are teachers saying?
4. Would there be interest in a student-facing AI literacy program that pairs with your teacher training?
5. Are you planning another fall conference? Could NGL contribute?

---

## What Not to Do
- Do not pitch software. This is about education programs.
- Do not oversell. He ran Apple Books and B&N Digital. Be genuine.
- Do not ignore his book background. He is a lifelong reader. Show intellectual curiosity.

---

## Best Case Outcome
- He sees NGL as the K-8 pipeline into Iona's AI education ecosystem
- Intro to the Gabelli Center or whoever runs teacher training
- Pilot program discussion: NGL runs a student workshop at Iona or Yonkers school
- Invitation to their next AI conference as a presenter

title: Proposal: AI Literacy Program at Mount Pleasant Library
content:
# Proposal: AI Literacy Program
## Mount Pleasant Public Library, Pleasantville, NY

*Prepared for Janet Green, Head of Youth Services*
*April 9, 2026*

---

## Program Overview

A 3-week AI literacy workshop series for students in grades 4-8. One session per week (Mondays), starting Fall 2026. Each session is a standalone experience so students can attend one, two, or all three.

---

## Format

- **Duration:** 3 sessions, once per week (Mondays)
- **Session length:** 90 minutes each
- **Audience:** Grades 4-8
- **Group size:** 10-20 students recommended
- **Season:** Fall 2026 (exact dates TBD with Janet)

---

## What Students Learn

Each session is hands-on. Students don't sit and listen. They build, question, and think critically about AI.

**Three core skills:**
1. **Prompt Engineering:** How to communicate clearly with AI through precise, thoughtful prompts
2. **Output Verification:** How to evaluate and verify what AI produces before trusting it
3. **Ethical Reasoning:** Understanding the responsibilities of using AI

**The "Different Means, Same End" approach:**
Whether a student loves art, music, coding, or entrepreneurship, they use their own interest as the path to AI literacy. The artsy kid designs with AI. The coder builds with AI. The quiet kid finds their angle. Everyone ends up with the same critical thinking skills.

---

## Session Structure (90 minutes)

| Block | Time | What Happens |
|-------|------|-------------|
| The Hook | 15 min | Live AI demos: impressive and flawed. Kids see what AI can and can't do |
| Socratic Discussion | 20 min | Hard questions: "Who made this? Is it biased? Why did it give that answer?" |
| The Build | 45 min | Students use AI tools to solve a creative challenge tied to their interests |
| The Debrief | 10 min | Students present work and reflect on where AI helped vs. got in the way |

---

## What We've Seen in Our Programs

Results from pilots across 3 CT libraries (Danbury, Ridgefield, Easton), grades 2-8:

- **Fear to Ownership:** Students arrived thinking AI was magic. By session 3-4, they were revising prompts and disagreeing with outputs
- **Students asked to stay:** Kids were disappointed when sessions ended
- **Thinking became visible:** Before-and-after mind maps showed measurable growth in clarity and confidence
- **Partner, not employee:** When students treated AI as a partner instead of a shortcut, they shifted from consuming answers to making decisions

---

## What the Library Provides

- A room with tables and chairs
- Wi-Fi access
- That's it. We handle everything else.

## What NGL Provides

- Full curriculum (designed, tested, refined across multiple programs)
- All instruction and facilitation
- Age-appropriate AI tools and interfaces (walled-garden, safe for youth)
- Materials and handouts
- Post-program summary for the library

---

## Investment

**$150-175 per session** (90 minutes)

3 sessions total: **$450-525 for the full program**

This is in line with what Mount Pleasant typically pays for outside programming.

---

## Our Track Record

- **3 library programs** completed (Danbury, Ridgefield, Easton)
- **50+ students** trained across grades K-8
- **50+ educators** trained at Woodstock Public Schools PD Day
- **Wooster School:** Student and faculty sessions
- **Founded at Babson College**

---

## Next Steps

1. Janet confirms Fall dates and preferred Mondays
2. We lock in the 3-week schedule
3. NGL builds a custom session plan for Mount Pleasant's audience
4. Library promotes the program to families
5. We show up and deliver

---

*Ryan Vincent*
*Co-Founder, Next Generation Learners*
*ryanvincent@nextgenerationlearners.com*

title: Eiker Jacome — Ad Tracking Tool (Hyros Alternative)
content:
# Eiker Jacome — Ad Tracking Tool Project
*April 8, 2026 — Ryan Vincent*

---

## Source
Instagram DM from Eiker Jacome.

## The Ask
Build a custom ad tracking / attribution platform that replaces Hyros. Hyros is the current industry standard for ad tracking but has two major problems: inaccurate data and expensive pricing ($300-1000+/mo).

Eiker wants a tool that:
1. **More accurate attribution** — track the full journey from ad click to sale without the data gaps Hyros has
2. **More granular data** — not just "this ad made $X" but deeper breakdowns (which ad, which audience, which funnel step)
3. **Backend client success stats** — not just front-end metrics (clicks, leads) but what happens after the sale: client retention, results, lifetime value
4. **Cheaper than Hyros**

## Follow-Up Questions (Need Answers Before Scoping)
1. Which ad platforms? (Meta, Google, YouTube, TikTok?)
2. What's the sales funnel? (Landing page → call booking → sale? Direct purchase?)
3. What payment/CRM stack? (Stripe, GoHighLevel, Kajabi, ClickFunnels?)
4. What specifically is broken with Hyros? (Misattribution? Missing sales? Overcounting?)
5. What does "client success" mean in his business? (Retention, completion, revenue milestones?)
6. Is this for his own use or a SaaS product he'd sell?

## Scope Estimate
- **Internal tool** (just for Eiker): weeks — connect ad APIs + Stripe + dashboard
- **SaaS product** (sellable to others): months — needs multi-tenant, onboarding, billing, pixel infrastructure

## Status
Discovery — waiting on follow-up answers from Eiker.

title: Walter Meeting — KPI Tracker Discovery
content:
# Walter Meeting — KPI Tracker Discovery
*April 8, 2026 — Ryan Vincent*

---

## Meeting Summary

Met with Walter to discuss building a custom KPI tracker for his real estate wholesaling business. Walter is an acquisitions specialist who cold-calls daily and needs a system to track his numbers. He currently has no dedicated KPI software.

**Outcome:** Agreed to build a custom KPI tracker. Ryan committed to delivering a proposal + working demo. Walter will send his 10 specific KPI criteria.

---

## What Walter Needs

### Core KPI Metrics (Cold Calling)
- Dials per day (he does 75-81+ calls/day)
- Sellers who answered
- Seller conversations
- Offers made (written)
- Rejections
- Contracts sent
- Contracts signed
- Contracts closed
- Follow-up calls
- New deals created

### Dashboard Features
- Manual daily input (log numbers after each calling session)
- Daily, weekly, and monthly rollups
- Auto-calculated conversion rates between stages
- Pipeline view: active deals, projected profit, expected close dates
- Visual charts and trend analysis
- Clean, fast, mobile-friendly

### Pipeline/Deal Tracking
- Number of active deals under contract
- Projected profit per deal
- Expected closing date
- Deal progress percentage
- Total projected pipeline profit

---

## Walter's Current Workflow

- Uses a CRM with color-coded lead statuses (white=no contact, red=dead, purple=active, green=under contract, orange/yellow=interested not ready)
- Calls through leads daily, 75-81+ dials per day
- Has a script system (intro, follow-up) but mostly goes off the cuff
- Has automated text follow-up system (monthly texts to leads)
- First deal closing June 3rd
- Very persistent, relationship-driven approach — calls same leads every day

---

## Market Opportunity

Walter explicitly said this has broad market potential:

> "Every single person in the wholesale industry, in the life insurance industry... you can sell to salesmen. If you pitch them on how important your numbers are... you can go pitch it to your solar friends, your door-to-door friends, and then add one for wholesalers."

- **Will pay for it** and refer others
- **Has a TikTok** he can use to promote
- **Knows other wholesalers** and sales professionals who need this
- Competitor reference: friend built a KPI system using **Base44** — we need to match or exceed it

---

## NGL Traction Shared

- Software launched ~6 days ago
- 8-10 meetings booked with $10M+ businesses
- 12 leads from recent outreach
- First paid program running today at Ridgefield Library
- Taught 50 educators with high engagement
- Another proposal meeting tomorrow

---

## Next Steps

1. Walter sends his 10 specific KPI criteria
2. Ryan builds proposal page + working software demo
3. Ryan delivers proposal walkthrough + live demo
4. If approved, template for wholesaling/sales KPI market
5. Explore broader sales vertical: wholesalers, insurance, solar, door-to-door

---

## Strategic Value

This is NGL's entry into the real estate/sales vertical. If the KPI tracker works for Walter, it becomes a template product for any sales role — same data structure, different titles. Recurring revenue opportunity.

title: Session: Research Agent, Activity Feed, Jarvis Sharing
content:
# Session Summary — April 7, 2026

## What We Built

### Research Agent (NGL Dashboard)
- Built an AI research agent that scans education news for NGL
- Pulls from 5 sources: EdSurge, K-12 Dive, THE Journal, and 2 Google News queries
- Claude analyzes articles and only saves high-value insights (potential customers, competitors, grants, policy changes)
- Runs daily via Vercel cron + manual "Run Now" button on dashboard
- Competitor monitoring for Khanmigo, Magic School AI, SchoolAI, Synthesis

### Activity Feed Redesign (NGL Dashboard)
- Completely rebuilt the activity feed with avatars, stats bar, better descriptions
- Added external activity sync — Gmail sent/received emails, GitHub commits + PRs, Vercel deployments
- Tracks across 3 GitHub repos: NGL site, Ryan's Jarvis, Brayan's Jarvis
- Auto-syncs every 5 minutes, manual sync button, live indicator
- Inbox filter to see incoming emails separately
- Stats bar: Ryan today / Brayan today / Total today / All time
- Live activity ticker on dashboard home screen

### Jarvis Workspace Sharing
- Pushed Ryan's Jarvis to ngl-team/ryan-jarvis (private)
- Created ngl-team/brayan-jarvis repo for Brayan
- Activity feed now tracks Jarvis updates from both workspaces
- Created team-summary skill so Ryan can ask "what's Brayan doing?"
- Sent Brayan instructions to push his Jarvis

### Fixes
- Fixed Vercel build failure (DB client created with undefined URL at build time)
- Fixed Vercel deploy blocked by Hobby plan cron limit (changed to daily)
- Fixed dashboard crash from duplicate `let` declaration
- Fixed research agent returning 0 results (switched to Haiku, parallel fetches, maxDuration=60)
- Fixed research runs not attributed to the user who triggered them

## Deployment Notes
- Vercel auto-deploy was broken — had to use `npx vercel --prod` CLI deploys
- Hobby plan limits: 1 cron job (daily only), 10s default function timeout
- Added `export const maxDuration = 60` for research and sync endpoints

## Still Pending
- Brayan needs to push his Jarvis to ngl-team/brayan-jarvis
- VERCEL_TOKEN, VERCEL_PROJECT_ID, VERCEL_TEAM_ID env vars for deployment tracking
- CRON_SECRET env var for automated research cron
- Gmail send re-authorization (readonly only right now)

title: Wooster School — Teacher Reconnection Strategy
content:
# Wooster School — Teacher Reconnection Strategy

**Date**: 2026-04-06
**Status**: Planning

## Background
Ryan attended Wooster School (private high school). He wants to reconnect with three former teachers:
- Mrs. Marino (via text)
- Mr. Rexford (via email)
- Mr. Smollin (via email)

## Strategy
**Phase 1 — Reconnect (now)**
- Reach out as a former student, not as a salesperson
- Update them on what he's been building with NGL
- Ask genuine questions about what they're experiencing in the classroom
- Understand their pain points and what they're struggling with

**Phase 2 — Learn (after initial conversation)**
- Dig deeper into their specific challenges
- Understand what tools they wish existed
- Map their pain points to potential software solutions

**Phase 3 — Offer (only if it fits)**
- If NGL's existing tools solve a real problem they have, offer a pilot
- If they don't, use the insights to build something that does

## Key Principle
This is relationship-first, not sales-first. These are people who taught Ryan. The goal is genuine reconnection and learning, with business as a natural byproduct if the fit is right.

title: Session: Gmail CRM Scanner, Lesson Planner, Follow-Up Emails
content:
# Session Summary — April 5, 2026

## What Got Done

### 1. Gmail CRM Scanner

- Found 40 NGL contacts from 232 sent emails
- 17 replied, 23 unanswered
- 39 library contacts, 1 other

### 2. Shared CRM Connection

- Identified 9 high-interest contacts (people showing real engagement — meetings, calls, active dialogue)
- All 9 were already in the CRM (Brayan loaded them), tagged as source: "brayan"
- **Action needed:** Brayan needs to update source to "ryan" on these 9 contacts. The API PUT endpoint accepts requests but doesn't persist the source field change. Either fix the API or update directly in the database:
  ```sql
  UPDATE contacts SET source = 'ryan' 
  WHERE email IN (
    'jgreen@mountpleasantlibrary.org',
    'alandry@hopkintonma.gov',
    'mbeaudreault@westhartfordct.gov',
    'tgoggin@eastonlibrary.org',
    'jryan@westportlibrary.org',
    'sprosser@ryelibrary.org',
    'lmoser@irvingtonny.gov',
    'cconkey@watertown-ma.gov',
    'joule.bazemore@woosterschool.org'
  );
  ```

### 3. Matt Byrnes Identified
- "Mr. Burns" is actually **Matt Byrnes**, Head of School at Wooster School in Danbury, CT
- Email: matt.byrnes@woosterschool.org
- Already in shared CRM (Brayan added him)

### 4. Follow-Up Emails Drafted

- Saved to `artifacts/follow-up-emails.md`
- Ready to send tomorrow morning

### 5. Strategic Decisions Made
- **Libraries are OUT of the 100-school goal** — tracked separately
- Brayan is handling Mr. Roche follow-up (due Wednesday April 9)

### 6. Lesson Plan Partner Built & Deployed
- Built a new AI-powered tool directly into the NGL website (nextgenerationlearners.com/lesson-planner)
- Teachers can: set up classroom profiles, generate customized lesson plans via Claude AI, save and manage plans

## Message Sent to Brayan
> Hey Brayan — I just pushed a new feature to the NGL site. If you go to nextgenerationlearners.com and look at the navbar, you'll see "Lesson Planner." It's an AI-powered tool where teachers can set up their classroom (grade, subject, class size, special needs) and generate customized lesson plans instantly. It saves all their plans so they can come back to them. Right now Lesson Plan Partner is live, and Differentiation Engine, Student Feedback Writer, and Parent Email Partner are coming soon. One thing I need your help with: add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the Vercel environment variables so the AI generation works. I'll send you the key.

### NGL Dashboard Repo (next-gen-learners)

- `src/app/api/classrooms/route.ts` — Classrooms API
- `src/app/api/lesson-plans/route.ts` — Lesson plans list API

title: Follow-Up Emails — 21 Unanswered Library & Youth Contacts
content:
# Follow-Up Emails — Unanswered Library & Youth Program Contacts

*Generated: 2026-04-05*
*Template: 15-min call to explore program fit*

---

## 1. M. Borden — Falmouth Public Library
**To:** mborden@falmouthpubliclibrary.org
**Subject:** Re: Are Kids Learning AI the Right Way?

Hi,

I reached out about a month ago about our AI literacy program for youth. I know how busy things get at Falmouth Public Library, especially heading into summer planning season.

title: Strategic Blocker Plan: Time Management & Environment
content:
# Strategic Blocker Plan: Time Management & Environment

*Created: 2026-04-04*
*Owner: Ryan*
*Horizon: 90 days (by 2026-07-04)*

---

## The Real Problem

You're not unproductive because you're lazy. You're unproductive because your environment doesn't protect your time, and the people around you aren't on the same clock. Every day is a negotiation between your future and your present social life — and without a system, the present wins more than it should.

There are two levers to fix this: **structure your time** and **upgrade your environment**.

---

## Lever 1: Time Structure

### The Core Principle
You can't manage what you don't track. Right now, time is invisible. The first move is making it visible.

### Actions

**Week 1 — Audit**
- For 7 days, log every hour. What did you actually do? Use your phone's notes app, a simple spreadsheet, or any tool. Don't judge it — just capture it.
- At the end of 7 days, look at where time leaked. You'll see the pattern.

**Week 2 — Design your ideal week**
- Block your non-negotiable work hours first. Minimum 4 focused hours per day on business-building (outreach, product, strategy). Treat these like appointments you cannot cancel.
- Assign specific tasks to specific blocks. "Work on business" is not a block. "Call 5 schools" or "build feature X" is a block.
- Schedule fun. Skimboarding and surfing go on the calendar too — fixed times, not open-ended. This removes the guilt and removes the infinite availability.

**Daily anchor habit**
- Every morning: write 3 things you will accomplish today before the day is done.
- Every night: score yourself. Did you do them? Why or why not?
- This takes 5 minutes and builds the discipline feedback loop.

**Tool recommendation**
- Restart Monday.com with your business partner, but keep it simple: 3 columns — To Do, In Progress, Done. One board for business development (school outreach), one for product.
- Alternatively, use a simple daily note system if Monday feels heavy. The tool matters less than the habit.

---

## Lever 2: Environment Upgrade

### The Core Principle
You become the average of who you spend time with. Right now your average doesn't include enough builders. That's not a character flaw in your friends — it's just math. Fix the inputs.

### Actions

**Find your people (ongoing)**
- Identify 2-3 local founders or builders you respect or want to be around. Even one person in your city who is building something changes your baseline.
- Go to founder meetups, startup events, and entrepreneurship groups in your area. You were just at a conference — that's the right instinct. Keep going to those.
- LinkedIn outreach to founders in edtech or education technology who are 1-2 years ahead of you. Ask for a 20-minute call. Most will say yes.

**Create accountability**
- Find one accountability partner — a person who is also building — and do weekly check-ins. 15 minutes every Monday. What did you commit to last week? Did you do it? What are you committing to this week?
- This makes your goals real and witnessed. It changes the social pressure from "come hang out" to "did you hit your number?"

**Protect your time from friends without burning relationships**
- You don't have to disappear. You have to be unavailable during work blocks and fully present during scheduled downtime.
- Practice saying: "I'm heads down until 4pm, let's link after." Simple. No guilt required.
- Friends who respect you will respect the boundary. The ones who don't — that's information.

**Longer-term: move toward the environment you want**
- If your 90-day goal is to land 2-3 clients and talk to 100+ schools, that performance will start opening doors to different rooms — accelerators, founder communities, investor networks. Stay focused on the 90-day numbers and the environment will start to upgrade itself.

---

## 90-Day Milestones

| Week | Focus | Goal |
|------|-------|------|
| 1 | Audit | Know exactly where your time is going |
| 2 | Design | Ideal week built, work blocks protected |
| 3-4 | Outreach begins | First 20 school conversations |
| 5-8 | Momentum | 50+ school conversations, 1 serious prospect |
| 9-12 | Close | 100+ conversations, 2-3 clients signed |

**Non-business goal running in parallel:** Daily physical routine locked in by end of week 2. Body and focus are infrastructure — they're not separate from the business goal.

---

## The One Thing

If you only do one thing from this plan, do this:

**Design your ideal week this weekend and live it for 14 days straight.**

Everything else — the environment, the clients, the body — follows from having protected time to actually work. Time is the first domino.

---

*Review this plan in 30 days. What's working? What isn't? Update accordingly.*

title: School Outreach Tracker — 100 Schools in 90 Days
content:
This is what I see right now on consolecloud.google.com.# School Outreach Tracker

*Last updated: 2026-04-04*
*Goal: 100+ school conversations by 2026-07-04*
*Product: Personal AI assistant for school superintendents — handles admin work, emails, meetings, minutes, and board prep so they can spend more time in the classroom.*

---

## Progress

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| Total contacts reached | 1 |
| Conversations had | 0 |
| Proposals / pitches sent | 1 |
| Clients signed | 0 |

---

## Pipeline

| # | Contact | Role | School | State | Method | Date Contacted | Status | Next Action | Notes |
|---|---------|------|--------|-------|--------|----------------|--------|-------------|-------|
| 1 | Mr. Roche | Principal | Woodstock | CT | Warm intro (Brayan) + conference | 2026-04-04 | Pitch sent — awaiting response | Follow up by 2026-04-09 if no reply | Met at conference 2026-04-03. Very interested. |

---

## Status Definitions

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| Identified | Have their contact info, not yet reached out |
| Contacted | Email sent, no response yet |
| Responded | They replied — conversation is open |
| Meeting scheduled | Call or meeting booked |
| Pitch sent | Formal proposal or demo sent |
| Negotiating | Active back-and-forth on terms |
| Closed | Signed client |
| Not interested | Passed for now — revisit later |

---

## Cold Email Queue

People and schools to reach out to next. Move to Pipeline once contacted.

| Contact | Role | School | State | Source | Notes |
|---------|------|--------|-------|--------|-------|
| Mr. Burns (first name TBD) | Head of School | Wooster School | CT | Former school | Top decision-maker. Get first name before outreach. |
| Chris Pernone | Vice Principal | Wooster School | CT | Former school | Strong existing relationship. Coordinate with Brayan first. |
| Diane Martin | Curriculum Director | Wooster School | CT | Former school | Pitch alongside Burns and Pernone as a package. |

---

## Notes & Learnings

*Add notes here as you learn what works and what doesn't in your outreach.*

- **2026-04-04:** Conference at Woodstock generated strong interest from teachers. Mr. Roche (principal) was most engaged. In-person events are high-signal.

---

## 90-Day Milestone Targets

| Date | Target |
|------|--------|
| 2026-05-04 (30 days) | 25 schools contacted, 1 client signed |
| 2026-06-04 (60 days) | 60 schools contacted, 2 clients signed |
| 2026-07-04 (90 days) | 100+ schools contacted, 2-3 clients signed |
